appeared_in_branch.descriptionHtml: A previously created code scanning alert appeared in another branch. This can happen when a branch is merged into or created from a branch with a pre-existing code scanning alert.
appeared_in_branch.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to code scanning alerts in a repository. For more information, see "About code scanning" and "About code scanning alerts." For information about the API to manage code scanning, see "Code scanning" in the REST API documentation.

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Code scanning alerts" repository permission.

bodyParameters.name: alert
bodyParameters.description: The code scanning alert involved in the event.

childParamsGroups.name: created_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that the alert was created in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: dismissed_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that the alert was dismissed in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: dismissed_comment
childParamsGroups.description: The dismissal comment associated with the dismissal of the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: dismissed_reason
childParamsGroups.description: The reason for dismissing or closing the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: fixed_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time that the alert was fixed in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

childParamsGroups.name: html_url
childParamsGroups.description: The GitHub URL of the alert resource.

childParamsGroups.name: analysis_key
childParamsGroups.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. For example, in GitHub Actions this includes the workflow filename and job name.

childParamsGroups.name: category
childParamsGroups.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed.

childParamsGroups.name: environment
childParamsGroups.description: Identifies the variable values associated with the environment in which the analysis that generated this alert instance was performed, such as the language that was analyzed.

childParamsGroups.name: ref
childParamsGroups.description: The full Git reference, formatted as refs/heads/<branch name>.

childParamsGroups.name: state
childParamsGroups.description: State of a code scanning alert.

childParamsGroups.name: number
childParamsGroups.description: The code scanning alert number.

childParamsGroups.name: description
childParamsGroups.description: A short description of the rule used to detect the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: A unique identifier for the rule used to detect the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: severity
childParamsGroups.description: The severity of the alert.

childParamsGroups.name: state
childParamsGroups.description: State of a code scanning alert. Events for alerts found outside the default branch will return a null value until they are dismissed or fixed.

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis alert.

childParamsGroups.name: version
childParamsGroups.description: The version of the tool used to detect the alert.

bodyParameters.name: commit_oid
bodyParameters.description: The commit SHA of the code scanning alert. When the action is reopened_by_user or closed_by_user, the event was triggered by the sender and this value will be empty.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: ref
bodyParameters.description: The Git reference of the code scanning alert. When the action is reopened_by_user or closed_by_user, the event was triggered by the sender and this value will be empty.

bodyParameters.name: repository
bodyParameters.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Webhook payloads contain the repository property when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

bodyParameters.name: sender
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.
